title: Dungeon Recovery Company procedural visual validation
operationalUse: Capture Play Mode visual evidence and automatically detect camera, UI, target visibility, and missing shader issues before human playability review.
userGoal: Prove the procedural recovery job is visibly playable enough for manual review by capturing screenshots and visual assertions.

expectedDocuments.expected: Dungeon Recovery Company procedural recovery job requires visual validation before human playability review.
Generated visual probe captures camera-rendered PNG screenshots, camera framing state, UI visibility, target visibility, and material diagnostics.
Evidence records screenshot paths, camera framing assertions, UI visibility, target visibility, magenta ratio, and missing shader status.
Production State Graph includes operational visual validation requirements and evidence mapping.

validationChecks.expected: Generated visual validation probe and procedural scripts compile with zero compile errors.
Visual validation probe captures screenshots and returns Passed/Failed/Blocked assertions.
Screenshot files exist for spawn, mouse look, near target, interaction prompt, and job complete checkpoints.
Magenta/missing shader ratio remains below threshold and missingShaderSuspected is false.
Visual validation evidence is exported and linked to REQ-DRC-VIS-* and AC-DRC-VIS-*.

passCriteria: Unity Bridge health endpoint is reachable.
Procedural scene exists and can be opened.
Compile status passes after generated visual probe script is imported.
Five visual checkpoints produce PNG screenshots.
Screenshot width and height are greater than zero.
First-person camera exists and is valid.
Camera is not inside a wall or outside the generated dungeon.
Mouse-look equivalent rotation changes camera pose.
Progress UI, seed UI, interaction prompt, and Job Complete UI are visible at their checkpoints.
A recovery target is visible at the near-target checkpoint.
Magenta pixel ratio stays below threshold.
Public Release Ready remains No and humanReviewRequired remains true.
